hub_subtitle: Inglês para o trabalho — do aperto de mão ao acordo fechado, em 7 aulas

lesson.menu_title: Small Talk at the Trade Fair
lesson.menu_desc: Walk up to a booth, start a conversation and swap contacts with an international exhibitor -- 32 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 3 -- Small Talk at the Trade Fair
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Ziláudio | Lesson 3 -- Small Talk at the Trade Fair
lesson.grammar_point: questions and could for polite requests

listenings.text: Good afternoon, and welcome to the second day of the International Agriculture Fair. A quick word before you start. The best business here does not happen in the big talks -- it happens at the booths, in small conversations, face to face. So walk around, say hello, and ask questions. Every exhibitor has a story and a product. Do not be shy. When you meet someone interesting, ask for a business card, and give them yours. That is how a contact becomes a deal. The networking lunch is at one o'clock, next to hall two. Coffee is free all day. Have a great day, and good conversations.

Hi Zilaudio, this is Emma from GreenField Seeds. It was very nice to meet you at our booth this morning. Thank you for the good conversation about your farm in Mato Grosso. I have your business card here, and I would like to talk more about our soybean seeds. Could we meet again tomorrow, before the fair closes? I am at booth number twenty, next to the main entrance, all day. If tomorrow is difficult, no problem -- just send me an email and we find another time. Thank you again, and enjoy the rest of the fair.
